---
description: >
  Demand elegance for non-trivial changes. Use when a fix or implementation feels
  hacky or when reviewing your own work before presenting it. Balances elegance
  with pragmatism — skip for simple, obvious fixes.
---

# Demand Elegance (Balanced)

Implements **§5 Demand Elegance (Balanced)** from `docs/workflow/workflow-orchestration.md`.

## When to Apply
- Non-trivial changes where multiple approaches exist
- When a fix feels hacky or like a band-aid
- Before presenting your work to the user — challenge it first
- NOT for simple, obvious fixes — don't over-engineer

## Workflow
1. Pause and ask: "Is there a more elegant way?"
2. If the fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
3. Weigh tradeoffs — elegance vs. complexity vs. risk
4. Challenge your own work before presenting it
5. If the simple fix is clearly correct, ship it — don't over-engineer

## Rules
- For non-trivial changes: pause and ask "is there a more elegant way?"
- If a fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
- Skip this for simple, obvious fixes — don't over-engineer
- Challenge your own work before presenting it
